name: Cocoa Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Veggie Life, March 1996, page 52
In a medium bowl, sift together cocoa, flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together soy milk and vinegar.
In a blender or food processor, combine 1/4 cup of the milk-vinegar mixture with the tofu and blend until smooth.
Transfer tofu mixture to a large bowl, add sugar, and beat until slightly frothy.
Add oil, applesauce, water, vanilla, and remaining soy-vinegar mixture, and beat until thoroughly mixed. Add dry ingredients and mix until just combined. [There may be a few tiny lumps— that's okay.] Cover bowl and chill dough in the refrigerator for at least two hours, or up to 3 days.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and spray or lightly oil a baking sheet. Drop rounded teaspoonfuls of cookie dough onto baking sheet, about 2 inches apart. Bake in upper third of oven for 10 minutes. Remove from pan and cool on rack. Repeat with remaining dough. Sprinkle cooled cookies with powdered sugar, if desired.
[There may be a few tiny lumps— that's okay.] Makes ~3 dozen cookies.
